Tool Call: retrieve("specificity of HLA B27 in diagnosing Ankylosing Spondylitis")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered information, my reasoning is reinforced regarding the significance of HLA-B27 in diagnosing Ankylosing Spondylitis. Approximately 50%–92% of people with Ankylosing Spondylitis carry the HLA-B27 marker, emphasizing its high specificity. Furthermore, research shows that even though other conditions might occasionally exhibit HLA-B27 positivity, it is much rarer outside of Ankylosing Spondylitis. Therefore, while other diagnostic indicators such as lumbar movement limitations or increased ESR levels could suggest the possibility of Ankylosing Spondylitis, confirming through HLA-B27 testing significantly enhances accuracy. Thus, I maintain agreement with the original conclusion that \boxed{A} represents the most reliable singular biomarker for diagnosing Ankylosing Spondylitis specifically.
